ideation: This animation sequence simulates the act of looking out at the ocean from a passenger ship.  It starts with a relaxed posture, then moves to arms resting on a railing, and ends with a gesture of looking out at the horizon. The left arm will primarily do the 'leaning' action while the right arm will be used to signal 'looking'. The use of both arms creates a more natural and engaging animation. Frame 1 shows arms at rest. Frame 2 shows the robot leaning on a railing. Frame 3 shows a looking-out-at-the-horizon pose. Frame 4 shows a slight return to a more neutral position.

frames.beat: arms rest at sides, calm before the voyage
both arms lean forward, resting on a ship's railing
left arm rests, right hand shields eyes looking out
both arms relax slightly, the view remains
